name: Al Kooper
description: American songwriter, record producer and musician
abstract: Al Kooper (born Alan Peter Kuperschmidt; February 5, 1944) is an American songwriter, record producer, and musician. Throughout much of the 1960s and 1970s he was a prolific studio musician, including playing organ on the Bob Dylan song "Like a Rolling Stone", French horn and piano on the Rolling Stones song "You Can't Always Get What Yo…

works_created.t: I Love You More Than You'll Ever Know

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Brecker
about_quotes.evidence: Al Kooper — we had a band meeting and he didn’t like the idea of adding another singer so he quit and at the same time I announced that I was going to join Horace Silver.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Brecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 12, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Fred Lipsius
about_quotes.evidence: Dick Halligan and myself as arrangers for Blood, Sweat & Tears and the original, not quite a leader, Al Kooper
about_quotes.doc_title: Fred Lipsius interviewed by Monk Rowe. Boston, Massachusetts, July 25, 2018
